memory.recent_lessons_md:
### 2026-06-05 Market-Close

**GLD as portfolio hedge confirmed:** On a -1.89% S&P 500 day with tech -5%, GLD gained +$3.36/sh. Portfolio outperformed SPX by +1.91% today — defensive positioning works on risk-off days. The 20-day underperformance streak was broken by GLD holding/gaining when tech sold off. LESSON: Even underwater positions (GLD -1.8% from entry) can serve their portfolio role on the right kind of day.

**Re-scoring at key market junctures:** The watchlist (MU 7.67, AMD 7.17, PLTR 7.0) dropped to 6.5/6.0/6.17 today due to Macro 3/10 and Technical 5/10. These scores legitimately don't meet the 7.0 threshold, so no MOC entries were required. LESSON: Deployment bias applies to ≥7 fresh scores; if the score itself drops below threshold due to changed market conditions, the obligation doesn't apply.

**Stale GTC orders are a risk on bounces:** AMD $524/$508, MU $1,033/$1,020, PLTR $150.74 limits are all ABOVE today's closing prices and could fill on Monday's expected bounce. OPERATOR must cancel these before market opens Monday to avoid overpaying for positions. Better bracket prices are available.

**Tech selloffs create better entries:** AMD $493 (vs $524 prior target = $31 better), MU $963 (vs $1,033 = $70 better), PLTR $141 (vs $150 = $9 better). If Macro recovers Monday (Nasdaq bounce after -3% is historically common), all three re-qualify at 7+.

### 2026-06-04 Midday

**GLD price estimate was significantly wrong:** Prior portfolio.md had GLD at $432-435. Actual June 3 close: $407.91. Stop $397.92 is now 3.2% away (improved from 2.4%). Always use confirmed data.

**AVGO post-earnings structure:** Even with Goldman $525 PT, technical requires 2/5 indicator confirmation before entry. AVGO now at $395 — two sessions after earnings miss. Still forming base.

**Sympathy selloffs improve entries:** AMD/MU down due to AVGO news but thesis unchanged. Today: AMD $493, MU $963 = significantly better entries than prior stale targets.
